composition.Programmer 1:
Implement the backend code in app.py
- Develop the main backend Python application using Flask.
- The website should be accessible via local port 5000, with the first page accessible at route '/' (Dashboard).
- Implement routing for all seven pages  Dashboard, Artifact Catalog, Exhibitions, Exhibition Details, Visitor Tickets, Virtual Events, Audio Guides.
- Implement file read/write operations for all data files in the 'data' directory using plain text, starting from the first line, without encryption.
- Implement business logic for exhibitions, artifacts, tickets, events, audio guides, and user interactions.
- Ensure all routes correspond to frontend pages.
- Frontend files to be supported  dashboard.html, artifact_catalog.html, exhibitions.html, exhibition_details.html, visitor_tickets.html, virtual_events.html, audio_guides.html.
composition.Programmer 2:
Develop all frontend HTML templates in the 'templates' directory
- Complete the frontend code for all seven pages  dashboard.html, artifact_catalog.html, exhibitions.html, exhibition_details.html, visitor_tickets.html, virtual_events.html, audio_guides.html.
- Use the specified element IDs as per the requirements document.
- All navigation URLs in the frontend must use Flask routes, not direct HTML file names.
- Dynamically render data passed from backend routes.
composition.Programmer 3:
Develop static assets in the 'static' directory
- Create CSS styles in css/styles.css for consistent UI design.
- Create JavaScript in js/scripts.js for client-side interactivity, including button actions and filters.
- Optionally manage audio files in static/audio/ for audio guides.
